title: CEO turnover is near record highs, and more experienced execs are making for the exits
body: Oops, something went wrong Tim Cook announced plans to step down as Apple CEO this week, with longtime Apple insider John Ternus taking the role on Sept. 1. Among his peers at the top of the corporate ladder, this development makes Cook's decision to step away now far from unique . Data from consultancy Russell Reynolds found that in the first quarter of 2026, there were 77 incoming CEOs named across the 13 indexes tracked by the firm, which includes the S&P 500, FTSE 100, and Germany’s DAX 40, among others. This marked the highest first quarter total since at least 2018. In 2025, CEO departures hit a record high. Cook will also leave Apple after 15 years at the helm.
